---
title: "Science Communication (Under Construction)"
---

Science communication is a central part of my work as a scientist. I enjoy translating research across disciplines and audiences through public talks, documentary filmmaking, outreach, art–science collaborations, and community engagement. My work aims to make marine science accessible while fostering dialogue between researchers, artists, educators, and the public.

## Featured Projects

:::::: grid
::: card
### Documentary Filmmaking

Served as lead filmmaker for a documentary project during a month-long oceanographic expedition aboard R/V *Falkor (too)*. Planned and captured footage documenting daily life aboard a research vessel, hydrothermal vent exploration, and scientific field operations.
:::

::: card
### Art & Science

Collaborated with sculptors, visual artists, and scientists through the Synergy Experience, developing interdisciplinary projects exploring relationships between art, ecology, and environmental research. Work was featured in multiple gallery exhibitions.
:::

::: card
### Public Engagement

Organize and facilitate public art-and-science communication events drawing approximately 200–600 attendees, bringing together researchers, artists, musicians, and community members around environmental themes.
:::
::::::

## Invited Talks & Conference Presentations

### Deep-Sea Biology Society (2021)

**Presentation**

*Alviniconcha hessleri symbiont population structure by vent field and not by lifestyle.*

### URI Graduate School of Oceanography Student Seminar

**2021 — Presentation**

Comparative genomics of deep-sea hydrothermal vent snail *Alviniconcha hessleri* symbionts.

**2020 — Presentation**

Genome content and characteristics of a deep-sea hydrothermal vent snail symbiont.

### Society of Women in Marine Science Symposium (2020)

**Invited Speaker**

Helping undergraduate students navigate graduate school applications.

### Smithsonian Environmental Research Center

**2020**

Guest presentation.

**2016**

Acoustic Behaviors of the Bocon Toadfish.

### URI Microbiome Symposium (2020)

Poster

*Assembling the Genome of a Chemosynthetic Symbiont from Deep-Sea Hydrothermal Vent Snail Alviniconcha hessleri.*

### Global Sustainable Soundscape Network (2015)

Presentation

*A Preliminary Study of Soundscape Analysis as a Measurement of Ecosystem Health.*

### DePaul University

-   DePaul Art Museum (2015) — Lecture
-   School of Music (2014) — Lecture
-   Philosophy Department (2014) — Lecture
-   Science Symposium (2014, 2015) — Posters

## Outreach & Community Engagement

-   Deep Ocean Stewardship Initiative (DOSI), Science Communication (2021–2022)
-   BayInformed (2018–present)
-   URI Office of Marine Programs, Outreach Scientist (2019)
-   Inner Space Center Volunteer (2019)
-   Rhode Island Museum of Science & Art (2019)
-   Conservation Genetics Workshop Volunteer (2016)

## Community Art & Science

-   Synergist Vol. I (2018)
-   Synergist Vol. II Co-organizer (2019)
-   Synergy Experience (2024)
